title: centaurea margaritacea subsp. donetzica
intro: Centaurea margaritacea subsp. donetzica, also known as the Donetz cornflower, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Centaurea margaritacea subsp. donetzica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and promote the elimination of excess fluids in the body. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat various health conditions, such as urinary tract infections and skin issues.
